DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column3: Define the backend architecture, create the project folder structure, set up the FastAPI application, establish main API routes and share the API contract with all teams.
column4: Working FastAPI base project with organised folder structure, main.py, basic routes (/ and /health), and BACKEND_PLAN.txt shared with team

column3: Create the backend route files for player, crowd, and test functionality and implement the initial API endpoints required for frontend and integration testing.
column4: Route files created (players, crowd, test) with working endpoints /api/test, /api/players, and /api/crowd

column3: Create player_client.py and crowd_client.py returning mock JSON responses for player and crowd data. Add USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag to .env so mocks can be toggled on/off without code changes.
column4: player_client.py and crowd_client.py created with structured mock JSON responses and USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag supported

column3: Implement config.py to load all environment variables from .env using python-dotenv. Fill in .env with local dev values including USE_MOCK_SERVICES=true as default.
column4: .env and config.py completed with all required environment variables including mock service toggle

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column2: CORS + Route Wiring + Frontend Checkpoint 1 + Contract Push
column3: Configure CORS in main.py to allow frontend on localhost:3000. Wire all completed routes into main.py. Share BACKEND_PLAN.txt and Swagger URL with frontend team. Send contract template to player and crowd teams and request confirmation of ports, endpoints, and response format. Review and merge all week 1 PRs.
column4: CORS configured, all routes wired, API contract shared with frontend team, contract confirmation requested from player and crowd teams

column3: Implement schemas/auth.py (RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, AuthResponse, UserResponse) and schemas/health.py (HealthResponse).
column4: Pydantic schemas ready for use in auth routes

column3: Connect player_client.py and crowd_client.py to the API routes. Verify mock responses return correctly through the gateway when USE_MOCK_SERVICES=true.
column4: Mock services integrated and returning structured data through the routes

column3: Implement database.py (PostgreSQL async connection via SQLAlchemy) and models.py (users and jobs table definitions). Set up local PostgreSQL and confirm connection works.
column4: Working database connection with users and jobs tables created

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column3: Implement auth/hashing.py (bcrypt password hashing) and auth/jwt.py (JWT token creation and validation). Implement RBAC dependencies get_current_user and require_admin. Chase up player and crowd teams for contract confirmation if not received. Review all PRs.
column4: Working auth system with password hashing, JWT tokens, and role-based access control. Contract status known.

column3: Implement routes/auth.py with POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login, and GET /auth/me endpoints.
column4: Auth endpoints working and returning correct responses

column3: Implement schemas/jobs.py with JobSummary, JobDetail, JobListResponse, and UploadResponse Pydantic models.
column4: Job schemas ready for use in upload and job routes

column3: Add global exception handler to main.py to catch all unhandled errors and return clean JSON. Add request logging middleware to log all incoming requests.
column4: All unhandled errors return clean JSON responses, all requests logged

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column3: Implement routes/upload.py to validate video format, save to uploads/, create a job in the database, and return job_id immediately. Wire route into main.py. Verify frontend can connect to /health and /auth/login — confirm CORS and connection working.
column4: POST /upload working, job created in DB, frontend connection verified

column3: Implement routes/jobs.py with GET /status/{job_id}, GET /jobs, GET /jobs/{job_id}, POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry, and DELETE /jobs/{job_id} with RBAC enforced.
column4: All job endpoints working with correct role-based access control

column3: Implement process_video() background task using asyncio.gather to call player and crowd services in parallel. Respect USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag — use mock clients if true, real clients if false. Handle partial results if one service fails. Update job status in DB to done, partial, or failed.
column4: Background processing working with mock toggle support, job status updated correctly

column3: Write tests/test_auth.py (register, login, token validation) and tests/test_health.py.
column4: Auth and health tests passing

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column3: Finalise confirmed contracts with player and crowd teams. Update BACKEND_PLAN.txt and .env with real service URLs. Decide whether to proceed with real integration or stay on mocks based on other teams' readiness. Review all PRs.
column4: Contracts finalised, integration decision made, .env updated accordingly

column3: Write tests/test_upload.py (file format validation, job creation) and tests/test_jobs.py (job status, pagination, retry) using mocked service responses.
column4: Upload and job tests passing with mocked services

column3: If player team contract is confirmed, update player_client.py to call real player service. Keep USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag — real client used only when flag is false. If contract not confirmed, keep mock and document blockers.
column4: player_client.py supports both mock and real mode via USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag

column3: If crowd team contract is confirmed, update crowd_client.py to call real crowd service. Keep USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag — real client used only when flag is false. If contract not confirmed, keep mock and document blockers.
column4: crowd_client.py supports both mock and real mode via USE_MOCK_SERVICES flag

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column2: Core Frontend Integration Checkpoint 3 + Demo Mode Prep
column3: Run full pipeline locally with both real and mock modes. Verify frontend can hit /upload and /status/{job_id} end-to-end. Confirm demo can run in Mode A (real services) or Mode B (mock fallback). Review and merge all integration PRs.
column4: Both demo modes verified — Mode A with real services and Mode B with mock fallback

column3: Handle timeout errors from player and crowd services, return 400 for bad video formats, and return partial or failed status when a service is down.
column4: All error cases handled with correct HTTP status codes and clean responses

column3: Test POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry with a real partial failure scenario. Verify asyncio.gather handles one service failing correctly and only the failed service is retried.
column4: Retry working correctly in real partial failure scenarios

column3: Run all tests against real services if available. If not, run against mocks. Fix any failures and document integration status clearly.
column4: All tests passing, integration status documented

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column2: Buffer — Fix Integration Issues or Start Docker
column3: If real service integration is behind, use this week to resolve blockers and stabilise integration. If integration is on track, begin Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml setup. Review all PRs.
column4: Integration stable or Docker setup started depending on progress

column2: Buffer — Fix Failing Tests or Docker Config
column3: Fix any remaining test failures or integration issues. If on track, swap .env URLs from localhost to Docker service names and begin Docker testing.
column4: Tests passing or Docker config started

column2: Buffer — Service Client Fixes or Docker Testing
column3: Resolve any service client issues from integration. If on track, test player_client.py and crowd_client.py with Docker service names.
column4: Service clients stable or Docker-ready

column2: Buffer — Test Suite Fixes or Docker Test Run
column3: Fix any outstanding test failures. If on track, run the full test suite inside Docker and fix any Docker-specific issues.
column4: Test suite stable or passing inside Docker

column3: DESCRIPTION
column4: OUTPUT

column3: Write README.md with local and Docker setup instructions. Do final review of all code. Run full end-to-end demo — attempt Mode A (real services) first, fall back to Mode B (mock) if needed. Coordinate final demo run with frontend and all teams.
column4: Complete README, all code reviewed, demo confirmed working in at least one mode

column3: Verify /docs looks clean and accurate. Add any missing response descriptions to all endpoints.
column4: Clean and accurate Swagger UI at /docs

column3: Add request and response logging to all service clients. Clean up any leftover debug code.
column4: All service client calls logged, codebase clean

column3: Run the complete test suite one final time. Ensure all tests pass. Remove any leftover debug code.
column4: All tests green, codebase clean and demo-ready
